violations.text: An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area as well as shared dishwashing area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

Molluscan Shellfish tags did not have all of the required information. Last used dates were lacking. Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

Butter was observed held at 126 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

Liquid eggs were observed held at 65 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC placed in cooler for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Inspector observed chowder cooling in a container in the prep unit, in containers greater than four inches deep with lid. PIC removed and placed container in ice bath. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

Inspector observed lobster meat thawing in a sink of stagnate water. PIC placed items in container with cool running water during inspection. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: 1) under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); 2) completely submerged under running water; 3) as part of the cooking process; or 4) in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

Fish was observed thawed/stored in ROP packaging in the walk-in and cold hold drawer. PIC removed fish from packaging. Thawing ROP seafood product: 1) ROP seafood must be removed from packaging prior to thawing under refrigeration; 2) by submerging under cold running water after removing the seafood product from the packaging; OR 3) by submerging under cold running water with packing intact and removing product from packaging immediately once thawed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026
Tongs were observed stored on hand-sink splash guard. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026
The ice wand was observed stored unprotected in the freezer between uses. Utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026
Inspector observed staff drying dishes with cloth in the shared main dishwashing area. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the food cart have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026
The ceiling in the dry storage area (above freezer unit) is in poor repair (previous water damage). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2026

The floors in the dry storage area and floor drain under ice bar have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. PIC removed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/28/2026
